text:
IRON MOUNTAIN, Mich. (WLUC) - Drivers on US-2 in Iron Mountain may have noticed a group of 60 people marching south towards the Iron Mountain Police Department on Tuesday morning.

The Iron Mountain Community Torch Run returned, raising money and awareness for the Special Olympics.

The group of Special Olympics athletes, organizers and parents was led by torch bearer and IMPD Deputy Director Adam Ray.

“To be able to give back to the Special Olympics community is just amazing,” Ray said. “To see the expression and appreciation on the athletes faces to be able to compete in the events is pretty awesome.”

Michigan State Police Trooper Geno Basanese brought up the rear of the walkers. He’s joined the community torch run since it was revived in 2014.

He says there is plenty of support from people around Dickinson County.

“It just shows, for how long we’ve been doing it, is the community support,” Basanese said. “And it’s been great, because at the end of the day, it’s for the athletes, they do amazing. It’s just the monetary assistance really helps.”

Dickinson-Iron Intermediate School District Special Education Supervisor Lisa Anderson brought students from the ISD to walk. She says the students appreciate being a part of the torch run.

“So, our student’s love the torch run,” Anderson said. “They love racing against each other, they love participating with the law enforcement officers, and they just love getting out and being together.”

The walk raised almost $1,430 for the Special Olympics.

To find resources to donate online, go to theTorch Run website.

title: Community Torch Run raises money, awareness for Special Olympics
description: The walk raised almost $1,430 for the Special Olympics.
keywords: Iron Mountain,Michigan State Police,Iron Mountain Police Department,Special Olympics
